nativeRange.habitat: Shallow coastal reef environments, rocky substrates, and sandy areas in the photic zone
nativeRange.biotope: Marine benthic; anchors via rhizoids into substrate; siphonous green macroalgae typical of warm, well-lit reef zones

avoidWith: tanks with no light schedule control

imageCandidates.notes: Primary product image from Mosaic Macros listing. Only known trade image for this unidentified Caulerpa sp.

summary: A distinctive Caulerpa with succulent, thick lobed blades reminiscent of a fern frond — an unidentified species available exclusively through Mosaic Macros. Fast-growing, beginner-friendly, and allelopathic; banned in California.
careNotes:
Lobe-Bladed Fern Caulerpa is a trade name applied by Mosaic Macros to an unidentified Caulerpa sp. with distinctive succulent, thick lobed blades described by the seller as growth comparable to Caulerpa mexicana. No species-level identification has been published; this entry remains at genus level. Mosaic Macros is the sole known hobby trade source.

This algae is sold in cuttings of 2–3" (4–5 blades) or 4"+ (6+ blades). It is suitable for both display tank use (as an ornamental specimen) and refugium placement for nutrient export.

ALLELOPATHY: Like all Caulerpa species, this macroalgae is considered to produce secondary metabolites including caulerpin and caulerpenyne. These compounds have been documented to inhibit or stress nearby corals and competing algae. It is generally advised to maintain physical separation from sensitive SPS corals; running activated carbon in the system is a commonly recommended precaution. Refugium placement limits direct allelopathic exposure to display corals.

GOING SEXUAL / REPRODUCTIVE CRASH: Under stress — particularly from nutrient crashes, disrupted photoperiod, or rapid temperature swings — Caulerpa species can undergo holocarpy, converting the entire thallus to gametes and effectively dying. This releases organic matter and gametes into the water column, creating a severe acute water-quality risk (ammonia spike, oxygen depletion) capable of crashing a reef tank. Recommended mitigations: run refugium lighting on a 24-hour cycle to suppress reproductive triggering; harvest regularly to keep the colony in active vegetative growth; maintain stable moderate nutrient levels rather than running nutrients to zero; immediately remove any specimen showing white/translucent fronds or turbid gamete release.

INVASIVE CONCERN: The Caulerpa genus includes several documented marine invasive species (notably C. taxifolia in the Mediterranean, C. racemosa in various locations). All Caulerpa are banned for sale in California. Captive specimens must never be released into natural waterways.

WATER PARAMETERS: Standard reef parameters are generally advised — 22–26°C, salinity SG 1.023–1.025, pH 8.1–8.4, alkalinity 8–12 dKH. Parameters are extrapolated from genus-level Caulerpa standards consistent with sibling entries (caulerpa-prolifera, feather-caulerpa) in the absence of species-specific published data.

GENERAL CARE: Tends to thrive under high reef lighting (5,000K or higher, 8+ hours photoperiod, or 24-hour continuous for refugium use). Tolerates moderate water flow; gentle tumbling works well in a refugium chamber.

UPON ARRIVAL: Fronds may appear white or translucent after dark shipping due to chloroplast displacement. This is normal; coloration typically recovers within a few hours of light exposure.
breedingNotes: Propagates readily via fragmentation — severed frond pieces have been reported to establish as new plants. Vegetative tip growth along the stolon is the primary growth mode in captivity. Sexual reproduction (holocarpy) is destructive and should be actively prevented (see careNotes).

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole known hobby trade source for this unidentified Caulerpa sp. Product page confirms succulent thick lobed blades, Caulerpa mexicana growth comparison, display suitability, California ban, and invasive designation. Specific water parameters and detailed husbandry not provided; extrapolated from genus-level Caulerpa standards.

additional.name: Caulerpa prolifera (sw-algae-004) — sibling entry
additional.notes: Cross-reference for genus-level husbandry standards: water parameters, allelopathy (caulerpin/caulerpenyne), holocarpy/crash behavior, invasive caveats, and propagation methods. Consistent with feather-caulerpa (sw-algae-035) sibling.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
